steps: Adding a constant to every value in a data set shifts all values equally.
The median is the 8th value (middle of 15) when ordered. Adding 56 to every value shifts the ordered list up by 56, so the median of B = median of A + 56 — it is greater.
Range = max − min. Adding 56 to both max and min: (max + 56) − (min + 56) = max − min. Range is unchanged.

• Choice A — 'medians equal, ranges equal': Median of B is 56 more than median of A, so they are not equal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'medians equal, range of B greater': Medians are not equal, and the range does not grow. ✗
  • Choice C — 'median of B greater, ranges equal': Adding 56 raises every value, so the median rises; max − min is unchanged. ✓
  • Choice D — 'median of B greater, range of B greater': The median comparison is correct, but the range stays the same. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
